zh.lifeSummary: 當Susanna Clark於1672年12月25日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德時，她的父親William Clarke24歲，母親Susanna Treat21歲。她於1694年11月9日與Josiah Bowen在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Susanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzannah
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

en.lifeSummary: When Susanna Clark was born on 25 December 1672, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, her father, William Clarke, was 24 and her mother, Susanna Treat, was 21. She married Josiah Bowen on 9 November 1694, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

Elle a épousé Josiah Bowen le 9 novembre 1694, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

ko.lifeSummary: Susanna Clark(이)가 1672년 12월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Clarke(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Susanna Treat(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1694년 11월 9일 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 Josiah Bowen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Susanna Clark родилась 25 декабря 1672 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени William Clarke было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Susanna Treat, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Josiah Bowen 9 ноября 1694 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
